description: Expose numeric relevance/UCB/combined scores in recall JSON output, and add a learning_supersessions join table with --supersedes flags and auto-filtering in recall.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
recall_learnings() and recall_learnings_with_backend() function signatures unchanged
next command and loop engine behavior unchanged
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at milestones — milestones must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Modifying recall_learnings() or recall_learnings_with_backend() signatures — next command and loop engine must remain unchanged
Adding new columns to base schema files in src/db/schema/*.rs — new tables go in migrations only

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test: recall_learnings_scored returns ScoredLearningOutput with relevance_score, combined_score, match_reason fields
Test: ucb_score is Some for --for-task recall, None for free-text recall
Test: combined_score == relevance_score for non-task recall (no UCB)
Test: output order matches combined_score descending (monotonicity invariant)
Test: JSON round-trip serialization preserves all score fields including None ucb_score
Test: unfiltered recall (no query, no task) returns relevance_score ~0.5 from FTS5 fallback
Known-bad discriminator: test that would PASS if scores were hardcoded 0.0 but FAIL with real scoring
Structural assertion: serde_json::from_value::<LearningSummary> succeeds with all new fields
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail until FEAT tasks implement)
userStories.description: Write TDD tests for recall_learnings_scored() and the new score fields on LearningSummary. Tests define the contract before implementation.
edgeCases: Recall with no backends matching (empty query, no task): relevance_score ~0.5 from FTS5 unfiltered fallback
UCB score for non-task recall: ucb_score is None, combined_score equals relevance_score
Vector backend unavailable (no Ollama): scores from other backends unaffected, no vector mention in match_reason

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compilation error — all fields are non-optional except ucb_score and match_reason

invariants: combined_score is monotonically non-increasing in output order
For task-based recall: combined_score == relevance_score * 100.0 + ucb_score
For non-task recall: combined_score == relevance_score and ucb_score is None
userStories.notes: Learning [1467]: Uniform (score, reason) tuples for multi-dimension scoring. Learning [1402]: VectorBackend score scale = cosine * 15.0. Learning [238]: Pattern scoring FILE_MATCH=10, TAG_CONTEXT=3.
qualityDimensions: Score fields must reflect actual internal scores that determined ranking order
If learning A ranks above B, then A.combined_score >= B.combined_score
recall_learnings() / recall_learnings_with_backend() must remain unchanged — no regressions
Follow existing (score, reason) tuple pattern from compute_score() in patterns.rs
No additional DB queries — score computation already happens, we preserve instead of discard
userStories.title: Initial tests for recall score output

acceptanceCriteria: Test: learning_supersessions table exists after migration v17
Test: v17 down migration drops table and reverts to schema version 16
Test: UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicate (old_id, new_id) pairs
Test: ON DELETE CASCADE removes supersession row when either learning is deleted
Test: self-supersession (old_id == new_id) is rejected with clear error
Test: superseding a non-existent learning ID is rejected
Test: superseded learning excluded from recall results
Test: --include-superseded flag includes superseded learning in results
Test: transitive supersession (A->B->C) correctly filters both A and B
Test: superseding a retired learning is allowed
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] until FEAT tasks implement)
userStories.description: Write TDD tests for the learning_supersessions table, --supersedes flag, and recall auto-filtering of superseded learnings.
edgeCases: Self-supersession (--supersedes <own-id>): reject with error 'A learning cannot supersede itself'
Superseding an already-superseded learning: transitive chains allowed, NOT IN catches all
Superseding a retired learning: allowed — relationship still meaningful for history
Duplicate supersession row: INSERT OR IGNORE / UNIQUE constraint handles gracefully

failureModes.cause: Migration run on pre-existing DB with learnings already present
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Migration succeeds — CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, no data migration needed

invariants: After v17 up + v17 down, schema_version is 16 and table does not exist
Recall without --include-superseded never returns a learning whose id is in learning_supersessions.old_learning_id
userStories.notes: Learning [833]: Don't add migration columns to base schema. Learning [835]: Base schema frozen at v0. Learning [1397]: Migration file structure pattern. Learning [348]: Down migrations for new tables use DROP TABLE (not column-level). Learning [1448]: Stub migration + #[ignore] enables TDD.
qualityDimensions: Supersession must be validated: both old and new learning IDs must exist
Self-supersession (old == new) must be rejected
CASCADE delete must clean up relationship rows
Base schema files (src/db/schema/*.rs) are frozen — new table lives only in migration
Migration follows established v1-v16 file pattern
userStories.title: Initial tests for supersession table and filtering

acceptanceCriteria: ScoredLearningOutput struct has: learning: Learning, relevance_score: f64, ucb_score: Option<f64>, combined_score: f64, match_reason: Option<String>
ScoredRecallResult struct mirrors RecallResult but with Vec<ScoredLearningOutput>
recall_learnings_scored() forks recall_learnings_with_backend body, preserving scores at what was line 115
For task-based recall: UCB scores computed and attached (refactored out of sort closure)
For non-task recall: ucb_score = None, combined_score = relevance_score
Existing recall_learnings() and recall_learnings_with_backend() unchanged — grep confirms identical
New types and function re-exported from src/learnings/mod.rs
All existing tests pass unchanged
userStories.description: Create ScoredLearningOutput and ScoredRecallResult types in src/learnings/recall/mod.rs. Implement recall_learnings_scored() that preserves scores instead of discarding them at line 115. Refactor rerank_with_ucb to expose UCB scores. Re-export from src/learnings/mod.rs.
userStories.notes: Key change: line 115 in recall/mod.rs currently does `scored.into_iter().map(|s| s.learning).collect()` discarding all scores. The new function preserves them. rerank_with_ucb (line 247) computes UCB inside a sort closure — extract to a separate function so scored variant can capture the values. Learning [1467]: Uniform (score, reason) tuples. Learning [273]: Decompose long scoring functions into per-dimension helpers.
qualityDimensions: Score fields must reflect actual internal scores that determined ranking order
recall_learnings() / recall_learnings_with_backend() must remain unchanged
Follow existing (score, reason) tuple pattern from compute_score()
UCB computation extracted to helper to avoid duplication with rerank_with_ucb
No additional DB queries — preserve scores already computed
userStories.title: Add ScoredRecallResult types and recall_learnings_scored() function

acceptanceCriteria: LearningSummary has: relevance_score: f64, ucb_score: Option<f64> (skip_serializing_if None), combined_score: f64, match_reason: Option<String> (skip_serializing_if None)
recall() calls recall_learnings_scored() instead of recall_learnings_with_backend()
from_recall_result() maps ScoredRecallResult to RecallCmdResult with score fields populated
format_text() shows scores after confidence line: 'Score: X.XX (relevance) | Y.YY (combined)'
format_verbose() uses actual match_reason from backend instead of hand-reconstructed reasons
JSON output: `task-mgr --format json recall --query X` includes relevance_score and combined_score
JSON output: `task-mgr --format json recall --for-task Y` additionally includes ucb_score
userStories.description: Add relevance_score, ucb_score, combined_score, match_reason to LearningSummary in src/commands/recall.rs. Switch recall() to call recall_learnings_scored(). Update format_text() and format_verbose() to show scores.
userStories.notes: Learning [710]: format_text naming convention in task-mgr. The existing format_verbose() at lines 204-283 reconstructs match reasons by re-checking title/content against query — replace with actual match_reason from backend. Data contract: ScoredRecallResult.scored_learnings[i] → LearningSummary { relevance_score, ucb_score, combined_score, match_reason }.
qualityDimensions: Score fields must reflect actual ranking order
Adding fields to Serialize struct is backward-compatible for JSON consumers
format_verbose() uses real match_reason, not hand-reconstructed reasons
Text output scores only when non-zero
userStories.title: Wire score fields into CLI recall output

acceptanceCriteria: CLI: Learn variant has #[arg(long)] supersedes: Option<i64>
CLI: EditLearning variant has #[arg(long)] supersedes: Option<i64>
LearnParams has supersedes: Option<i64> field
EditLearningParams has supersedes: Option<i64> field
main.rs: Commands::Learn dispatch passes supersedes through to learn()
main.rs: Commands::EditLearning dispatch passes supersedes through to edit_learning()
learn() implementation: after recording learning, if supersedes is Some: INSERT INTO learning_supersessions + UPDATE old learning confidence to low
edit_learning() implementation: if supersedes is Some: INSERT INTO learning_supersessions + UPDATE old learning confidence to low
Self-supersession (old_id == new_id) rejected with clear TaskMgrError
Non-existent old_id rejected with clear error
userStories.description: Add --supersedes <old-id> to Learn and EditLearning CLI variants. Wire through main.rs dispatch to CRUD layer. On supersession: insert row into learning_supersessions, downgrade old learning confidence to low.
userStories.notes: Learning [767]: Wiring new CLI commands requires edits in 3 locations. Learning [705]: Four integration points for new CLI commands. Learning [799]: Thread new CLI flag through clap+main+engine in one pass. Learning [706]: Learning struct lacks retired_at; use direct SQL for retired_at operations. Learning [728]: Confidence enum stored as lowercase strings in SQLite. The learn() function in src/commands/learn.rs uses LearningWriter (per CLAUDE.md chokepoint rule). Supersession INSERT + confidence downgrade happen AFTER the LearningWriter.record() call and AFTER LearningWriter.flush().
qualityDimensions: Supersession validated: both old and new IDs must exist
Self-supersession rejected with clear error message
LearningWriter chokepoint: new production learn paths must go through LearningWriter
Confidence stored as lowercase string in SQLite (learning #728)
Four integration points for CLI: commands.rs, main.rs, commands/*.rs, handlers.rs (learning #705)
userStories.title: Add --supersedes flag to learn and edit-learning commands

acceptanceCriteria: RetrievalQuery gains include_superseded: bool field (default false via Default trait)
RecallParams gains include_superseded: bool field (default false)
execute_fts5_query: adds supersession NOT IN subquery when include_superseded is false
execute_like_query: same supersession filter
execute_unfiltered_query: same supersession filter
load_learnings_with_applicability: same supersession filter in WHERE clause
VectorBackend: post-query Rust filter removes learning IDs present in learning_supersessions
CLI: Recall variant has --include-superseded flag
RecallCmdParams passes include_superseded through to LibRecallParams → RetrievalQuery
Superseded learnings excluded from default recall, included with --include-superseded

consumers.mitigation: recall_learnings() uses Default for RetrievalQuery which includes include_superseded: false — superseded learnings auto-filtered there too, which is correct
consumers.usage: Calls recall_learnings() which creates its own RetrievalQuery internally

consumers.mitigation: Same as above — default filtering applies

userStories.description: Add include_superseded: bool to RetrievalQuery (default false). Add NOT IN (SELECT old_learning_id FROM learning_supersessions) to FTS5 (3 query functions), Patterns (load_learnings_with_applicability), and Vector (Rust-level post-filter) backends. Add --include-superseded CLI flag to Recall command.
userStories.notes: Learning [1444]: Soft-archive queries need filters on all listing operations. Learning [282]: Separate filtering from scoring in retrieval logic. Learning [1465]: Tag-only learnings retrieval with EXISTS subquery. The supersession filter pattern is identical to existing retired_at IS NULL: add WHERE condition to every retrieval SQL. For vector backend, load superseded IDs first via simple SELECT, then filter in Rust after cosine computation.
qualityDimensions: Supersession filter added to ALL retrieval SQL locations (fts5: 3 functions, patterns: 1, vector: post-filter)
Default behavior changes: superseded learnings now excluded by default
include_superseded bypass works correctly
Existing retired_at IS NULL filters preserved alongside new supersession filter
No regression in recall speed — subquery on tiny table
userStories.title: Add supersession filter to retrieval backends

acceptanceCriteria: learnings list text output shows '(superseded by #N)' after learning title when superseded
learnings list text output shows '(supersedes #N)' after learning title when it supersedes another
learnings list JSON output includes superseded_by and supersedes fields when applicable
No annotation when learning has no supersession relationship
Query is efficient: single batch query for all displayed learning IDs, not N+1
userStories.description: Modify learnings list output to annotate superseded learnings with '(superseded by #N)' and superseding learnings with '(supersedes #N)'. Requires querying learning_supersessions table during list formatting.
userStories.notes: The learnings list command is in src/commands/learnings.rs. LearningSummary struct (different from recall's LearningSummary) has id, title, outcome, confidence, created_at, times_shown, times_applied. Add optional superseded_by: Option<i64> and supersedes: Option<Vec<i64>> fields.
qualityDimensions: Batch query for supersession annotations — no N+1
Annotations only shown when relationship exists
JSON output backward-compatible (new optional fields)
userStories.title: Annotate superseded learnings in learnings list output
